title: Upgrading from 4.016 to 4.024 introduces a verilation failure
body:
There appears to be a new limit introduced after 4.016 and possibly as late as 4.024.

Is this intentional?

This is generated Verilog where the ridiculously wide signal is ultimately narrowed down in synthesis optimization, so the physical signal is not as wide as the below.

```
Step #6: %Error: DataCache.v:9212: Unsupported: Width of number exceeds implementation limit: 8191'd0
Step #6:   assign _GEN_7533 = {{8191'd0}, state_slave_0_writeData};  
```
code_blocks:
Step #6: %Error: DataCache.v:9212: Unsupported: Width of number exceeds implementation limit: 8191'd0
Step #6:   assign _GEN_7533 = {{8191'd0}, state_slave_0_writeData};
